title: Singapore laptop math — pre-China bridge financing, Aug 2024
html:
TL;DR

  • A bare cash-flow note Yahir wrote in transit (Singapore) Aug 30 2024, right before flying into China.
  • State of finances: $530 in bank, $60 refund pending from "superlocal," needs a MacBook to start building.
  • Buy-laptop-in-Singapore decision because it'll be cheaper than China — estimates $950–1100. Check incoming for $1400 early next week.
  • The internal moral debate, transcribed: "I could get money from dad but this is not right. He needs it and you need to do this independently."
  • Solution: borrow $700 from Valeria short-term + use his old computer ($700 trade-in injection) → ends with $700 net after the new laptop.

Key quote

"I could get money from dad but this is not right. He needs it and you need to do this independently."

Why it matters in the graph

  • A clean ledger-snapshot of no runway discipline in action — Yahir running the math live, refusing dad's money, choosing Valeria as bridge instead. Same operational logic he uses in the 2025 09 23 how im scraping by gym-job period.
  • Sets the financial baseline entering China: $700 of working capital + a new MacBook + a Valeria debt to pay back. This is the seed of the china chapter story's economics.
  • "Need to do this independently" is Yahir's pride-as-strategy stance — surfaces also in 2024 10 30 thoughts on my future ("create our own grooves").

Open questions

  • Was Valeria paid back? On what timeline?
  • What was "superlocal" — a job, an app, a refund-processor?
  • Did the Singapore laptop purchase actually happen, and what model?

Wikilinks

valeria · no runway discipline · china chapter · m1 mac · 2025 09 23 how im scraping by · 2024 10 30 thoughts on my future · bridge income strategies
